setting.note: Mayfair drawing rooms and the opium dens of the docks, a few miles and an entire moral universe apart. Wilde is precise about the geography of respectability: Lord Henry keeps a house in Curzon Street, Dorian one on Grosvenor Square, and the road to ruin runs east to the quays past Blue Gate Fields.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Oscar Wilde, first published 1890; the author died in 1900.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States and in the United Kingdom and EU, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1900).
rights.attribution: Oscar Wilde, The Picture of Dorian Gray (1891). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The scarlet of Dorian's soul, which creeps from its hiding place "like a scarlet flame." Taken from the text and clears WCAG AA on paper.
